title: 10 best herbal creams for puffy eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with puffy eyes because they contain a unique blend of natural ingredients that work together to reduce swelling and inflammation. The cream's ability to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes allows it to tackle puffiness at its source, providing long-lasting relief from tired and fatigued-looking eyes. The gentle yet effective formula absorbs quickly into the skin, leaving the eye area feeling refreshed and rejuvenated, making it an ideal solution for combating puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum contains flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Adiantum, such as vitamin C and E, help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye puffiness.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Adiantum's ability to constrict blood vessels can help reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, leading to a reduction in puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds, roots of the maidenhair tree have been traditionally used to soothe and calm puffiness due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, bark extracts from the maidenhair tree help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation around the eyes, alleviating puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove from heat and let cool to around 180°f before whipping with an immersion blender for 2 minutes.
Pour into sterilized glass jars and allow to set at room temperature for at least 60 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with puffy eyes because of its natural cooling properties that quickly soothe and constrict blood vessels, reducing inflammation and fluid retention. The cream's menthol content also has a decongestant effect, helping to shrink the appearance of swollen eyelids. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds ease tension and relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing puffiness and leaving the area feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Caffeine: Constricts blood vessels, reducing puffiness by minimizing water retention and swelling.
Menthol: Cools and decongests the skin, reducing inflammation and easing tension around the eyes to reduce puffiness.
Vitamin K: Helps to reduce dark circles and discoloration under the eyes by promoting collagen production and improving circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help reduce puffiness and soothe tired eyes.
Leaves: High in menthol and other cooling compounds, peppermint leaves help constrict blood vessels and ease swelling around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out one hundred grams of distilled water and sixty grams of vegetable glycerin.
Combine the water and glycerin in a heat-proof mixing bowl and stir until dissolved.
Add twenty-five grams of dried peppermint leaves to the mixture and let steep for thirty minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Whip one hundred grams of beeswax and ten grams of coconut oil with an immersion blender until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with puffy eyes because of its unique blend of natural ingredients. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the Tasmanian blue gum plant, work to reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin around the delicate eye area. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the skin, reducing puffiness and leaving the under-eye area feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The menthol and cineole present in Tasmanian Blue Gum have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes.

Decongestant property: The eucalyptol in Tasmanian Blue Gum has a natural decongestant effect, which can help shrink blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation under the eyes, leading to reduced puffiness.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds present in Tasmanian Blue Gum, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help neutralize free radicals and protect the delicate skin around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and soothing irritated skin.
Barks: Containing bioactive compounds that help to calm and depuff the eyes, while also promoting healthy skin and tissue regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and rinse them with distilled water.
Steam distill the eucalyptus globulus leaves for 3 hours to extract their essential oil.
Mix 10g of beeswax pellets, 5g of coconut oil, and 1g of extracted eucalyptus essential oil together.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally until melted and smooth.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with puffy eyes because of their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal extract, known as calendula, soothes and calms the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing swelling and discoloration caused by fatigue, allergies, or sinus pressure. As a result, pot marigold creams can help to depuff and brighten the under-eye area, leaving it looking smoother, more radiant, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling, making it effective in alleviating puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
Vasodilatory properties: Pot marigold's ability to dilate blood vessels can help reduce fluid buildup under the eyes, which is often a cause of puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce puffiness and soothe irritated skin around the eyes.
Seeds: Contain natural oils with moisturizing properties, which can help hydrate and calm dry, puffy skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried calendula flowers in amounts of 2 cups and organic coconut oil in amounts of 1 cup.
Combine dried calendula flowers and coconut oil in a double boiler setup over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Add beeswax pellets in amounts of 1/4 cup and vitamin e oil in amounts of 2 tablespoons to the liquid mixture melting completely.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with puffy eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes. The herb's natural oils gently reduce puffiness and dark circles by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid retention. Additionally, chamomile's antiseptic and antibacterial properties help to eliminate any irritants or impurities that may be contributing to puffy eyes, leaving the area feeling refreshed, smooth, and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce puffiness by reducing swelling and soothing irritated skin around the eyes.
Antioxidant property: Chamomile cream's antioxidant property helps neutralize free radicals, which can cause eye puffiness and dark circles, promoting a smoother and more even-toned appearance.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile cream's soothing and calming properties help to reduce redness and irritation, making it easier for the skin around the eyes to relax and recover from puffiness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe puffy eyes.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds contain a high amount of fatty acids, which provide moisturizing properties to calm and nourish the skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20g of dried chamomile flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50ml of beeswax in a double boiler and melt the beeswax within 10 minutes.
Add 5g of the ground chamomile powder to the melted mixture and stir well for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature over a period of 30 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with puffy eyes because they are rich in antioxidants, anti-inflammatory properties, and natural astringents that work together to reduce puffiness and dark circles. The antioxidants combat free radicals, while the anti-inflammatory agents soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes. The natural astringent properties constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid buildup and swelling. As a result, tea creams provide a soothing and refreshing solution for puffy eyes, leaving them looking smoother, brighter, and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Many herbal teas, such as green tea and chamomile, contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce puffiness by soothing and calming the skin.

Constricting and decongesting properties: Herbal teas like peppermint and eucalyptus have a cooling effect on the skin, which helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup, thereby reducing puffiness.

Anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like calendula and lavender have anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate inflammation and itchiness associated with puffy eyes, promoting a smoother and more even complexion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and dark circles.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and promote circulation, alleviating puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves and dry them for at least 24 hours in a warm oven set at 150°f.
Combine one teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves with two tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar.
Add one tablespoon of beeswax and one tablespoon of vitamin e oil to the liquid and stir until melted and fully incorporated.
Pour the cream into airtight containers and allow it to cool and set for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The calming and soothing effect of lavender oil reduces swelling and irritation, allowing for a more radiant and rested appearance. Additionally, the cream's hydrating properties help to replenish moisture in the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles. By incorporating an herbal English lavender cream into your daily routine, you can wake up with refreshed, rejuvenated eyes that look bright and beautiful.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing swelling.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Sedative and calming properties: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can lead to a reduction in puffiness caused by fatigue, tension, or anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin around the eyes.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and fatty acids, seeds provide a natural emollient effect, hydrating and calming the delicate skin under the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground lavender powder with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and stir until fully incorporated.
Heat 50ml of coconut oil in a double boiler to 40°c, then slowly add the lavender-oil mixture while whisking constantly for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Allow the cream to mature for 24 hours before use, shaking the container occasionally to emulsify the ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with puffy eyes because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and swelling. The herbal extract in licorice cream, known as glycyrrhizin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to constrict blood vessels and alleviate puffiness. Additionally, licorice cream's moisturizing properties soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and discoloration. As a result, puffy eyes appear to deflate and become less noticeable, leaving the area looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce puffiness by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that causes blood vessels to dilate and lead to swelling.
Flavonoids: Act as powerful antioxidants that combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
Saponins: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness.
Rhyzomes: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing puffiness and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of glycerin and combine with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the glycerin dissolves completely.
Add 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Let the mixture steep at room temperature for 24 hours, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the cream base.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids, then pour the resulting cream into sterilized glass jars and refrigerate for up to 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng creams helps with puffy eyes because it is rich in adaptogenic properties that work to reduce inflammation and promote circulation. The cream absorbs quickly into the skin, helping to constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the delicate eye area, reducing swelling and dark circles. As a result, eyes appear smoother, brighter, and more refreshed, giving users a radiant and well-rested appearance.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Siberian Ginseng cream reduce swelling and redness caused by allergens, stress, or fluid retention, helping to alleviate puffy eyes.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Herbal Siberian Ginseng's vasoconstrictive properties help tighten blood vessels around the eyes, reducing excess fluid buildup and puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Siberian ginseng plant contain bioactive compounds that help reduce puffiness and inflammation, making them a popular choice for treating puffy eyes.
Barks: The barks of the Siberian ginseng plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eye tissue, reducing puffiness and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried eleutherococcus senticosus root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground eleutherococcus senticosus with 50ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 20ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir until it forms a smooth paste, taking approximately 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at 2°c to 8°c for at least 2 weeks, allowing for infusion.
After the infusion period, strain the cream through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with puffy eyes because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease tension. The soothing and moisturizing ingredients in these creams calm irritated skin and reduce puffiness, leaving eyes looking refreshed and revitalized. Additionally, marshmallow creams' ability to lock in moisture and hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes helps to depuff and smooth out fine lines and wrinkles, giving the appearance of more rested and alert eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow root contains mucilages that help reduce inflammation and swelling around the eye area, relieving puffiness and discomfort.

Moisturizing: The emollient properties of marshmallow root cream hydrate and soothe dry skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles and leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more refreshed.

Antioxidant: Marshmallow root is rich in antioxidants that help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and free radicals, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it effective in reducing puffiness and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Contain saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis leaves and flowers, dry them using shade drying or oven drying methods, within 2 hours of harvesting.
Combine 20g dried althaea officinalis leaves and flowers with 50ml distilled water in a heat-proof bowl, steeping for 30 minutes to release bioactive compounds.
Strain the herbal infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining 30ml of infused liquid.
Mix 15g beeswax pellets with 10ml coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until melted and well-combined to create a base mixture.

Combine the herbal infusion with 20ml glycerin and 20ml distilled water, heating gently over low heat while whisking until fully dissolved, then slowly adding the melted beeswax mixture while whisking continuously for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with puffy eyes because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and soothe irritation. The cream's natural ingredients, such as arctin and inulin, work to calm the delicate skin around the eyes, diminishing puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, burdock root has been shown to improve circulation, which can help to reduce fluid retention and alleviate puffy eyes caused by fatigue, stress, or allergies. By using an herbal burdock cream, individuals can enjoy a more radiant and rested appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes by reducing inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in burdock cream, such as polyphenols and phenolic acids, help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffy eyes.

Anti-edema properties: Burdock root has been traditionally used to reduce edema (swelling) and fluid retention, making it effective in helping to alleviate puffy eyes by reducing excess water buildup under the eyes.

Seeds: Burdock seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated skin, making them ideal for eye cream formulations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality arctium lappa roots and dried them in the shade for two weeks.
Grind 100 grams of dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Combine the powdered root with 200 milliliters of sweet almond oil and stir until well combined for ten minutes.
Heat the mixture to 60 degrees celsius and maintain temperature for one hour and fifteen minutes.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth and bottle immediately, using within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with puffy eyes because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of dandelion, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry taraxacum officinale leaves in shade for at least two weeks or until crispy.
Grind dried taraxacum officinale leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Combine powdered taraxacum officinale with 20% glycerin and 80% coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat mixture over low heat for approximately 30 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature before transferring it to airtight containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar creams helps with puffy eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosa rubiginosa petals and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the cleaned petals with 500ml of boiling distilled water and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl discarding the solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax pellets, 50g of coconut oil, and 25g of shea butter with the herbal infusion in a double boiler.
Whisk continuously for 10-15 minutes until the mixture thickens and emulsifies forming a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cucumber creams helps with puffy eyes because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and puffiness. The cooling effect of cucumber also constricts blood vessels, decreasing fluid buildup under the eyes. Additionally, herbal extracts in the cream, such as aloe vera and chamomile, soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing redness and irritation. Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids and flavonoids, they aid in reducing swelling and promoting healthy skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried cucumis sativus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the powdered cucumis sativus with 4 ounces of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and stir well for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 100°f to 110°f for 10 minutes, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and leaving behind 3 ounces of creamy herbal oil.
Whip 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of coconut cream until smooth and well combined, then add in the cooled herbal oil and whip until a rich and luxurious cream forms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with puffy eyes because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and swelling. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid accumulation under the eyes that can cause puffiness. remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean foeniculum vulgare leaves, drying them for at least 24 hours in a warm place.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the container daily to ensure even infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix 50 milliliters of the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes until smooth and creamy.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves at dawn or early morning when essential oils are most potent for maximum benefits.
Dry the thyme leaves in a warm oven at 40°c for 24 hours to preserve their potency and aroma.
Combine one part dried thyme leaves with two parts coconut oil and heat gently over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding solids after 20 minutes of steeping time.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for three hours before applying a thin layer under the eyes twice daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried salvia officinalis leaves measuring 2 tablespoons from a trusted herbalist or store.
Combine the dried leaves with 4 ounces of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 hours and then strain it using cheesecloth for separation.
Mix the infused oil with 8 ounces of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low heat until the wax melts completely taking approximately 10 minutes.

Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers then grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground achillea millefolium powder with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a small saucepan heating it over low heat for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it steep at room temperature for 4 hours or overnight allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids then add 10ml of beeswax and 5ml of vitamin e oil stirring until melted.
Pour the cream mixture into small sterilized jars filling them to the top and allowing them to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for puffy eyes are topical treatments that use natural ingredients to reduce swelling and inflammation around the eye area. These creams contain herbs such as cucumber, chamomile, and green tea, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the skin. Examples of herbal creams for puffy eyes include products containing aloe vera, witch hazel, and peppermint oil. Using these creams can significantly improve lives by providing long-lasting relief from puffy eyes caused by lack of sleep, allergies, or other factors, allowing individuals to look and feel more refreshed and rejuvenated throughout the day.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat puffy eyes with herbal creams is to use a formula containing anti-inflammatory herbs such as chamomile, peppermint, and witch hazel. Apply a thin layer of the cream gently under the peepers, allowing it to absorb fully before wiping away any excess. Reapply throughout the day as needed for optimal relief from puffiness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to puffy eyes that are treated with herbal creams are dark circles, fine lines and wrinkles, and skin discoloration. Herbal creams containing ingredients like caffeine, peptides, and antioxidants help reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels, stimulating collagen production, and neutralizing free radicals. They also soothe and moisturize the skin, leaving it looking smoother and more radiant. Examples of herbal creams for these issues include those made with chamomile, green tea, and gotu kola.
